hoses: Supply line from hydrant to Engine 181 intake
Attack Line A: 200 ft of 1.75-inch hose from pump panel discharge to front door firefighter
Attack Line B: 250 ft of 1.75-inch hose from rear discharge to Alpha-side firefighter
nozzles: Attack Line A: ChiefXD 185 fog nozzle, 185 GPM @ 50 PSI
Attack Line B: ChiefXD 185 fog nozzle, 185 GPM @ 50 PSI
sceneElements.elevation: Grade-level residential operation, 0 PSI elevation correction

problems.title: Total Flow Requirement
problems.question: What is the total flow requirement for the operation shown?
formulaBreakdown: Total flow = Line A flow + Line B flow.
problems.instructorExplanation: Both attack lines are flowing. Each ChiefXD 185 line requires 185 GPM, so the total flow requirement is 370 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count the hydrant supply line as an additional flowing line. The total flow is based on the attack lines being supplied.

problems.title: Attack Line A Friction Loss
problems.question: How much friction loss is present in Attack Line A?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
Line A uses 1.75-inch hose, so C = 15.5.
Line A hose length = 200 ft = 2 hundreds.
problems.instructorExplanation: Attack Line A is calculated by itself from the engine discharge to the nozzle. The friction loss rounds to 105 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add nozzle pressure when the question asks only for friction loss. Friction loss is calculated before nozzle pressure is added.

problems.title: Attack Line B Friction Loss
problems.question: How much friction loss is present in Attack Line B?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
Line B uses 1.75-inch hose, so C = 15.5.
Line B hose length = 250 ft = 2.5 hundreds.
problems.instructorExplanation: Attack Line B is longer than Line A, so it has more friction loss at the same flow. The friction loss rounds to 135 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use the Line A hose length for Line B. Each line must be calculated separately.

problems.title: Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Determine the required pump discharge pressure for Engine 181.
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
When multiple attack lines are supplied, the highest pressure line governs.
problems.instructorExplanation: Engine 181 must be set to supply the highest-pressure flowing attack line. Line B requires 185 PSI, so the pump discharge pressure is 185 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not average the two line pressures. The pump must be set for the line with the highest required pressure.

problems.title: Governing Attack Line
problems.question: Which attack line governs the pump discharge pressure for this operation?
problems.inputLabel: Enter 1 for Line A or 2 for Line B
formulaBreakdown: The highest pressure line governs pump discharge pressure.
problems.instructorExplanation: Line B has the higher required pump pressure because it has greater friction loss. Therefore, Line B governs the pump setting.
problems.explainMistake: Do not choose the closest or shortest line. The governing line is the line requiring the highest pump pressure.

problems.title: Gauge Reading With Lower-Pressure Line Shut Down
problems.question: If the lower-pressure attack line is not flowing while the other attack line remains in operation, what pump discharge pressure will display on the gauge?
formulaBreakdown: Line A is the lower-pressure line.
If Line A is not flowing and Line B remains flowing, Engine 181 must still supply Line B.
Gauge pressure = Line B pump pressure.
problems.instructorExplanation: The pump gauge displays the pressure being supplied by the pump. If the lower-pressure line is shut down but the higher-pressure line remains flowing, the pump still needs to supply 185 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not reduce the pump pressure to the lower line value when that lower-pressure line is shut down. The remaining flowing line still controls the pump setting.
